jokes.text: I don't even have a girl, much less a stupid one.
jokes.setup: George Michael is discussing his 'stupid girl problem'
jokes.punchline: He interprets 'stupid girl problem' as having a stupid girl rather than a stupid problem about a girl
jokes.explanation: Classic Arrested Development wordplay with perfect character-specific awkwardness. George Michael's literal interpretation fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: But if they ever had a child, it would be... because the girl was his cousin Maeby.
jokes.setup: Narrator suggests hypothetical child would be stupid
jokes.punchline: The real issue is incest, not intelligence
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection that subverts expectation. The audience thinks the joke is about intelligence, but it's actually about incest - much darker and funnier.

jokes.text: in the twilight of her face
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to be romantic about being with Lucille
jokes.punchline: Malapropism - says 'face' instead of 'life'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George Sr.'s tendency to mangle expressions while trying to sound sophisticated.

jokes.text: Oh, dear God. Send me back to prison.
jokes.setup: George Sr. desperately wanted to avoid prison
jokes.punchline: Now he's begging to go back after experiencing house arrest with Lucille
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal. The thing he feared most (prison) now seems preferable to his current situation.

jokes.text: As soon as Father came back, I've been like the Invisible Man. 'Hello! Can anyone see me?'
jokes.setup: Buster feels ignored since his father returned
jokes.punchline: He compares himself to the Invisible Man but gets the reference wrong - the Invisible Man wouldn't ask if people can see him
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Buster - he manages to bungle even a simple pop culture reference in a way that perfectly captures his neediness.

jokes.text: I've always loved those leathery little snappy faces. You certainly have a type.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests Buster get a turtle as a pet
jokes.punchline: Buster's description of turtles matches his attraction to older, domineering women
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight disguised as a throwaway line. Buster's type in women perfectly matches his description of turtles.

jokes.text: Oh, it's in the dishwasher. Your father and I were using it for something.
jokes.setup: Buster asks about his prosthetic hand
jokes.punchline: Implies his parents used his hand for sexual purposes
jokes.explanation: Masterfully inappropriate and delivered with Lucille's characteristic casual cruelty. The economy of language makes it even more disturbing.

jokes.text: My friskiness. Mama horny, Michael.
jokes.setup: Lucille announces what's 'back'
jokes.punchline: She announces her sexual appetite in the crudest possible way to her son
jokes.explanation: Iconic Lucille moment - she manages to be both sophisticated and completely inappropriate. The childish language makes it even more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Why do I eat breakfast before I come here?
jokes.setup: Michael has just heard way too much about his parents' sex life
jokes.punchline: He questions his basic life choices after this traumatic information
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction. Michael's question implies he regularly loses his appetite from family interactions.

jokes.text: A 'protest-acular.'
jokes.setup: Gob explains his magic trick is a protest
jokes.punchline: He creates a nonsensical portmanteau word
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob - he thinks combining words makes him sound clever but just sounds ridiculous. Perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Excuse me. I was just looking for a marker... A Magic Marker.
jokes.setup: Tobias appears during discussion of Gob's magic trick
jokes.punchline: He clarifies he needs a 'Magic Marker' (the pen) not magic marker
jokes.explanation: Typical Tobias literalism. He's oblivious to the context and focused only on his immediate needs.

jokes.text: That's where she keeps things she has no need for.
jokes.setup: Lucille tells them to put Gob's trick in Buster's room
jokes.punchline: She implies Buster is something she has no need for
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cruel Lucille moment delivered with her trademark casual brutality. The economy makes it even more cutting.

jokes.text: I so very much would like to be in your 'prostate-icular'
jokes.setup: Tobias wants to be in Gob's 'protest-acular'
jokes.punchline: He mispronounces it as 'prostate-icular'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias malapropism that accidentally creates a medical reference. His enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Guess I shouldn't have used a permanent marker.
jokes.setup: Tobias has drawn on himself with marker to look like Gob
jokes.punchline: He realizes his mistake only after it's too late
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to the Magic Marker setup. Tobias's poor planning creates a believable character moment.

jokes.text: I mean, what are we, Amish?
jokes.setup: Lindsay complains about the Volvo not having satellite radio
jokes.punchline: She equates lack of satellite radio with being Amish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay - she has no sense of proportion and thinks basic modern conveniences are necessities.

jokes.text: Lot of that was the coma.
jokes.setup: Lindsay says Michael and his wife weren't speaking toward the end
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals his wife was in a coma
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - Michael's deadpan delivery of this devastating information is both funny and tragic.

jokes.text: Yeah. I've heard your side of it.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his wife's silence was due to being in a coma
jokes.punchline: Lindsay implies there might be another side to the story of someone in a coma
jokes.explanation: Incredibly dark and perfect. Lindsay's response implies a coma patient might have another perspective. Brilliant escalation of the dark humor.

jokes.text: There you just had to shut your eyes and take it. Here you have to shut your eyes and give it.
jokes.setup: George Sr. comparing prison to his current situation
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about his relationship with Lucille vs. prison
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel structure that works on multiple levels. The prison/home comparison is both crude and insightful.

jokes.text: Andy Griffith. What, you never saw Matlock? Not a real attorney, Dad.
jokes.setup: George Sr. claims he got a big lawyer behind them
jokes.punchline: He hired the actor Andy Griffith thinking he's a real lawyer like his TV character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy showing George Sr.'s desperation and confusion of reality with TV.

jokes.text: For $20,000, he'll twice... lean forward and whisper something in your ear. Oh. White suit... That's extra.
jokes.setup: George Sr. explaining Andy Griffith's fee structure
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and expensive services for basically nothing, plus costume charge
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating absurdity. Each service gets more expensive for less actual value, culminating in the costume upcharge.

jokes.text: I thought that was the guy who played the Soup Nazi.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions George Sr. has a photo with Saddam Hussein
jokes.punchline: George Sr. confused Saddam Hussein with an actor from Seinfeld
jokes.explanation: Shows George Sr.'s complete disconnection from reality and pop culture confusion. The Seinfeld reference adds meta humor.

jokes.text: Not in every case.
jokes.setup: George Sr. says a photo like that can end your career
jokes.punchline: Cut to news footage showing the photo didn't hurt someone else's career
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual joke timing. The understatement combined with the visual evidence creates great irony.

jokes.text: Oh, they're polite and the men all sound gay... but they will rip out your heart
jokes.setup: George Sr. describing dangerous British people
jokes.punchline: He focuses on stereotypical traits while claiming they're dangerous
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s casual xenophobia mixed with genuine fear. The contradiction between 'polite' and 'dangerous' is funny.

jokes.text: Has anyone in this family ever even seen a chicken?
jokes.setup: Family members are making chicken noises and movements
jokes.punchline: Michael points out none of them know what a chicken actually looks like
jokes.explanation: Iconic running gag establishment. Meta-comedy that becomes more funny as the series progresses and characters continue to do bad chicken impressions.

jokes.text: It's more like a Lincoln. Oh, come on!
jokes.setup: Lindsay expects a Lexus car
jokes.punchline: She gets Lincoln bedroom furniture instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection gag. The visual payoff of cheap furniture instead of a luxury car works well.

jokes.text: There's no satellite radio, but there's a banjo in the closet. And watch out for live-ins.
jokes.setup: Michael describing Lindsay's new 'car'
jokes.punchline: The 'car' is actually a trailer home with occupants
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the trailer reveal. The banjo and live-ins paint a clear picture of what Lindsay got.

jokes.text: Lanes reverse! You're driving on the wrong side, you bloomin' idiot!
jokes.setup: Michael is in the British section of Orange County
jokes.punchline: British driver yelling about wrong-side driving in America
jokes.explanation: Good cultural gag establishing the British theme park area. Functional world-building comedy.

jokes.text: They're for British eyes only.
jokes.setup: Michael needs to check British business records
jokes.punchline: The clerk uses the episode title as an actual restriction
jokes.explanation: Clever title drop that works as both a James Bond reference and actual plot device. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: Perhaps if you're willing to lose 20 pounds.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if he looks British
jokes.punchline: The clerk suggests he'd need to lose weight to look British
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about British stereotypes. The clerk's matter-of-fact delivery helps.

jokes.text: What's the soup of the day, Mum? Bread.
jokes.setup: Lionel asking about soup of the day
jokes.punchline: The soup is just bread
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about the poor quality of British food. The deadpan delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Not her. Wait for it.
jokes.setup: Narrator says Michael sees the most beautiful woman he's ever seen
jokes.punchline: Camera shows wrong woman first, then reveals the real one
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection visual gag. The narrator's awareness of the misdirection adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Not... Not for sex. You're gonna think that I'm Jack the Ripper, right?
jokes.setup: Michael offers to pay Rita to go somewhere with him
jokes.punchline: He makes it worse by bringing up Jack the Ripper
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael awkwardness. His attempt to clarify makes the situation exponentially worse.

jokes.text: Then I shall let you live.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions Jack the Ripper killing prostitutes, Rita says she's not a prostitute
jokes.punchline: Rita plays along with the Jack the Ripper scenario
jokes.explanation: Rita's deadpan response shows she can match Michael's awkwardness with wit. Good character moment.

jokes.text: This is my worst hello. Let me try again.
jokes.setup: Michael has made a terrible first impression
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges it's his worst hello, implying he has a history of bad hellos
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Michael's self-awareness and social anxiety in a relatable way.

jokes.text: Well, lucky for you, my whole face is British.
jokes.setup: Michael needs someone with British eyes for the files
jokes.punchline: Rita offers her entire British face
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that advances the plot while being charming. Rita's delivery is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, yes... GMT. Everything here is on Greenwich mean time.
jokes.setup: Michael thought the office was open till 8:00
jokes.punchline: The clerk reveals they operate on London time in California
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist comedy about British commitment to authenticity even when impractical.

jokes.text: I like to think they teach me. - Like what? How to eat crayons, throw tantrums... - Oh, no. Not when I'm there. I'm very good.
jokes.setup: Rita says working with kids teaches her things
jokes.punchline: Rita's literal interpretation suggests she's learning child-like behaviors and is proud of being 'very good' at them
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work establishing Rita's mental limitations. Her innocent pride in being 'very good' is both sweet and revealing.

jokes.text: It's turning into one of my worst good-byes.
jokes.setup: Michael is struggling to end the conversation gracefully
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges this is matching his worst hello
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Michael's consistent social awkwardness. Character consistency is strong.

jokes.text: our paths shan't cross again. As you like it.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to say goodbye dramatically
jokes.punchline: He uses overly formal language, Rita responds with Shakespeare title
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both characters' intelligence and Rita's literary knowledge.

jokes.text: Are you gonna buy this time, or you just curious? I suppose I'm, uh, buy-curious.
jokes.setup: Shop owner asks if Tobias will buy something this time
jokes.punchline: Tobias creates accidental sexual innuendo with 'buy-curious'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - he creates accidental innuendo while being completely oblivious. The wordplay is brilliant and character-specific.

jokes.text: That's a pretty good chicken.
jokes.setup: Lindsay makes chicken noises
jokes.punchline: Michael admits this one is actually good, contradicting his earlier criticism
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the chicken running gag. Michael's admission adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Now, back off, you !@%$# ponce!
jokes.setup: British man threatening Michael
jokes.punchline: He uses very British insult 'ponce' while being threatening
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing British villain's politeness even while being threatening.

jokes.text: Dad was not kidding about their breath.
jokes.setup: Michael encounters the British thug
jokes.punchline: He confirms George Sr.'s earlier comment about British breath
jokes.explanation: Good callback that validates George Sr.'s earlier observation while adding humor to a threatening scene.

jokes.text: I'm thinking about calling it 'Free...' I don't know. 'Chicken.'
jokes.setup: Gob couldn't get rights to 'Free Bird' for his trick
jokes.punchline: Settles on 'Free Chicken' as a replacement, connecting to chicken running gag
jokes.explanation: Good connection of the magic trick to the chicken running gag. Gob's casual acceptance of the terrible name is in character.

jokes.text: I know what an erection feels like, Michael. No. It's the opposite. It's like my heart is getting hard.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to understand his feelings for Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: He describes love as the opposite of an erection - a hard heart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - he tries to understand emotion through physical sensation in the crudest possible way. The logic is absurd but character-perfect.

jokes.text: That's a woman's wig. - I was told it was a bob.
jokes.setup: Michael points out Tobias is wearing a woman's wig
jokes.punchline: Tobias's innocent confusion between 'bob' haircut and 'Bob' as a name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias wordplay confusion. His innocent misunderstanding of hairstyle terminology is classic character comedy.

jokes.text: That's a woman's wig. I was told it was a bob.
jokes.setup: Tobias appears wearing a wig
jokes.punchline: He defends the feminine wig by saying he thought 'bob' was a man's haircut
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias confusion about gender presentation. His innocent explanation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's a wonderful performance. You're a regular Brad Garrett.
jokes.setup: Michael accusing George Sr. of acting
jokes.punchline: He compares him to a supporting actor rather than a leading man
jokes.explanation: Mild insult comedy. Brad Garrett reference is specific but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That could be Buster. - Could be Gob.
jokes.setup: They're worried about the threat to 'somebody stupid' in their family
jokes.punchline: Both immediately think of Buster and Gob as the stupid ones
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy acknowledging the family's realistic assessment of Buster and Gob's intelligence.

jokes.text: I wouldn't. I'll make you blue. Rita was just talking about the finger paint... but Michael didn't know that.
jokes.setup: Michael asks Rita out, she says she'll make him blue
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals she means finger paint, not sadness
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on audience expectations. The narrator's clarification adds dramatic irony.

jokes.text: But instead he just sat in his car... and ate a whole thing of candy beans.
jokes.setup: Instead of doing something violent like Jack the Ripper
jokes.punchline: Michael's 'unsavory' behavior is just eating candy
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax. The contrast between murder and candy eating is absurd and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Where every day it rains... pennies from heaven!
jokes.setup: George Sr. planning to escape through storm drains
jokes.punchline: He imagines storm drains as idyllic with 'pennies from heaven'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing George Sr.'s delusional optimism about his escape plan.

jokes.text: Oh, I can just taste those meaty leading-man parts in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Tobias excited about potential acting roles
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm sounds sexual due to poor word choice
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias accidental innuendo. His complete obliviousness to the sexual implication makes it perfect.

jokes.text: An unlicensed Mary Poppins takes down another tourist. Is this the work of the Itsy Bitsy IRA?
jokes.setup: News report about Mary Poppins incident
jokes.punchline: Suggests terrorist organization made up of diminutive Irish people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining 'Itsy Bitsy Spider' with IRA. The absurdist concept of tiny terrorists is perfect.

jokes.text: A lot of people are probably here for the free chicken.
jokes.setup: Large crowd at Gob's magic show
jokes.punchline: Michael suggests they came for free food, not the show
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to chicken theme. Sets up the literal chicken reveal later.

jokes.text: Is that a chicken? Oh, my God. Dad, are you all right? I fooled his own son. I am a leading man.
jokes.setup: Gob's magic trick goes wrong
jokes.punchline: He accidentally uses a real chicken, but considers fooling his son a success
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the chicken running gag. Gob's pride in deceiving his son while using a real chicken is peak character comedy.

jokes.text: I am a leading man.
jokes.setup: Tobias has been desperate to be a leading man
jokes.punchline: Declares success while wearing a bald cap and having accomplished nothing
jokes.explanation: Good character delusion. Tobias's unearned confidence in his leading man status despite obvious failure.

jokes.text: Keeps him out of the kitchen.
jokes.setup: George Sr. has been captured after his escape attempt
jokes.punchline: Lucille treats his capture like a minor convenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - she reduces her husband's dramatic escape and capture to a domestic convenience issue.

jokes.text: They don't have a !@%$# prayer. Send the check to my accountant.
jokes.setup: Harry Hamlin appears as the prosecutor
jokes.punchline: He breaks character to discuss payment for his cameo
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about celebrity cameos. The sudden break from character is unexpected and funny.

jokes.text: Wow. She is way out of his league.
jokes.setup: George Sr. sees Rita
jokes.punchline: He confirms what Michael was thinking about himself
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment that confirms Michael's insecurities. The rewatch bonus comes from knowing Rita's secret.

jokes.text: For you see, Rita had a secret of her own.
jokes.setup: The narrator reveals Rita has been hiding something
jokes.punchline: Sets up major plot revelation for future episodes
jokes.explanation: Good setup for future episodes. The dramatic irony creates anticipation and makes rewatching funnier.
